verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes HSP70-1, the canonical inducible cytosolic chaperone. Under baseline conditions it is cytosolic/nuclear. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane under heat shock, other stress conditions, oncogenic transformation, and immunogenic/apoptotic cell death. Cancer cells in particular display HSP70 on their outer leaflet (cancer ecto-chaperone), a phenomenon exploited by therapeutic antibodies and CAR-T approaches targeting cell-surface HSP70. This is a classical example of stress/cancer-cell-state-induced outer-leaflet surface display of a nominally cytosolic protein.
